methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
DG Matrix is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Haroon Inam and Subhashish Bhattacharya, having raised $100M+. The sector composition includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers, notably Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation technique. Using total raised ($100M) as a proxy for enterprise value and applying a standard Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.0x yields an implied valuation of $200M. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible; while nine same-sector companies exist, insufficient datapoints met narrow-stage and valuation-tier filters (minimum three required), making an all-sector median unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $170M to $230M, centered on $200M. The primary uncertainty stems from methodological constraints: reliance on a single technique (stage-progression) rather than multiple independent approaches limits conviction. The absence of sufficiently close peer comparables in Climate & Energy at similar stage and scale leaves questions around whether the 2.0x step-up multiplier accurately reflects DG Matrix's specific risk and growth profile relative to market norms. Additional clarity on unit economics, technology differentiation, and revenue traction would strengthen the valuation.
